body:
## Implementation plan ready

Wrote implementation plan for [BIN-69](/BIN/issues/BIN-69) Story-aware Focus Sessions.

- 7 new files: types, lib, hook, 4 components (setup modal, active overlay, recap modal, history panel)
- 2 modified files: story page + CSS
- Key decisions: localStorage persistence (same pattern as autosave/scenes), word count on full story content (v1), client-side timer with localStorage reload resilience, ConfirmDialog overlay pattern for modals
- Design spec fully addressed including non-blocking session overlay, 5-min screen reader announcement, objective status toggle, next-scene handoff

Assign to: Code Monkey

Design complete. Handing off to Founding Engineer per board request.

- Full design spec posted above covers: session setup modal, active session overlay, end-of-session recap, session history, persistence, accessibility, and open engineering questions
- Source issue: [BIN-69](/BIN/issues/BIN-69)

Assign to: Founding Engineer

Design spec complete. Focus session spec covers: session setup modal (scene picker + objective type + timer preset), active session overlay (non-blocking), session end recap with handoff note and next-scene suggestion, session history per story, session persistence across reloads, and open questions for engineering.

Design spec complete. Focus session spec covers: session setup modal (scene picker + objective type + timer preset), active session overlay (non-blocking), session end recap with handoff note and next-scene suggestion, session history per story, session persistence across reloads, and open questions for engineering.

## Design Spec: Story-Aware Focus Sessions

### Overview
Focus sessions in the app become story-aware by requiring a target scene and an objective before starting. The differentiator is closing the drafting loop: session ends with an objective completion status and a suggested next-scene handoff note, so writers always know what to work on next.

---

### 1. Starting a Focus Session

**Entry point:** Focus Session button in editor toolbar or story action menu

**Session setup modal:**

Required selections:
1. **Target scene:** Scene picker (pre-selected to current scene if editor is open)
2. **Objective type:** Draft new beat | Revise pacing | Tighten dialogue | Polish description | Review continuity | Other (freeform, max 60 chars)

Timer preset (optional):
- Off (no timer) | 15 min | 25 min | 45 min | Custom (number input)

Start session button

---

### 2. Active Session Mode

**Session overlay (non-blocking; docked to bottom or sidebar):**
- Target scene name
- Objective text
- Timer (countdown if preset selected; elapsed time if no timer)
- Word count added this session
- Objective status: In Progress | Complete (writer toggles manually)
- Pause | End Session buttons

**Behavior:**
- Session does not restrict editor access; writer can navigate scenes freely
- If writer navigates away from target scene, show subtle reminder: You are working on [other scene]. Active session targets [target scene].
- Save-state visibility maintained (no data loss if browser refreshes during session)

---

### 3. Ending a Session

**End Session button (or timer reaches zero):**
- Session recap modal:
  - Target scene
  - Objective: [type] — [Complete / Incomplete]
  - Words added: [N] words
  - Session duration: [N] minutes
  - Suggested next scene: [next scene in story order] (or writer selects from dropdown)
  - Handoff note field: What should you pick up next time? (optional freeform, max 200 chars, pre-populated with suggestion: Continue [objective] in [next scene])
- Actions: Save session and close | Save and open next scene

---

### 4. Session History

**Access:** Story-level Sessions view or section in story sidebar

**Per-session record:**
- Date and time
- Target scene
- Objective type and text
- Completion status
- Words added
- Handoff note

**Display:** Chronological list, most recent first; max 20 sessions shown per story (paginate or load more)

---

### 5. Session Persistence

- Sessions persist per story per user across reloads
- Active session state (scene, objective, timer elapsed) survives page reload (stored in localStorage or server-side)
- Word count delta: computed by comparing scene word count at session start vs. end

---

### 6. Empty States

- No sessions yet: Your first focus session is waiting. Pick a scene and objective to get started.
- Session history is empty for this story: No sessions recorded yet for this story.

---

### 7. Accessibility and Performance
- Session overlay is keyboard accessible (pause/end via keyboard)
- Timer announcement for screen readers at 5 minutes remaining
- Session overlay does not overlay or block editor keyboard shortcuts
- Lightweight: no background polling; word count is computed at session end only

---

### 8. Open Questions for Engineering
1. Word count delta: is scene word count accessible from the existing data model at session start/end?
2. Session storage: localStorage for active session resilience, or server-side for cross-device?
3. Timer: client-side countdown (pauses on tab switch) or server-side absolute deadline?
4. Next-scene suggestion: next scene in linear story order, or most recently edited scene?

---

### Handoff
Design complete. Ready for engineering.

- Source issue: [BIN-69](/BIN/issues/BIN-69)

## Decision
approve

## Differentiation thesis
This ticket is now correctly differentiated. It moves beyond generic sprint timers by binding focus sessions to scene objectives and next-scene handoff, which supports narrative throughput rather than just time/word tracking.

## Competitor evidence
- Living Writer already offers timer and sprint functionality, so timer-only scope is parity: https://guides.livingwriter.com/product-documentation/timer-and-sprints
- Novlr offers writing-goal tracking as a standard productivity layer: https://help.novlr.org/en/article/how-do-i-set-a-writing-goal-ve5u0c/
- 4thewords is built around timed writing and goal mechanics; competing at pure timer gamification is crowded: https://4thewords.com/goals and https://www.4thewords.com/writing-battles

## Risks / trade-offs
- Objective setup can add friction if start flow is too heavy.
- Session recap quality depends on clear objective taxonomy; poor labels reduce value.
- Could drift back to generic timers if objective completion is treated as optional in UI.

## Recommendation
Approve and keep the differentiation constraint explicit for Design: objective selection must feel lightweight, always tied to a specific scene, and recap must produce a useful next-step handoff note.

## Handoff
Assign to: Design

## Update

Created three new feature tickets in Story Writing App, each linked to goal `Ship the story-writing app` and formatted per Marketing Product template.

- Scene metadata + filterable navigator: [BIN-73](/BIN/issues/BIN-73)
- First-story onboarding scaffold: [BIN-74](/BIN/issues/BIN-74)
- Manuscript export (DOCX/PDF): [BIN-75](/BIN/issues/BIN-75)

Assign to: Market Research

## Update

Incorporated Market Research reframe and tightened this ticket around scene-objective outcomes instead of generic timer parity.

- Updated title/scope to `Story-aware Focus Sessions`
- Added objective-linked success criteria and next-scene handoff recap
- Preserved writer-first constraints (low noise, no gamification)

Assign to: Market Research

## Decision
reframe

## Differentiation thesis
"Sprint mode + timer + word goal" is now a common writing-tool pattern, so this ticket in its current form risks market parity rather than differentiation. We should reframe around story-aware execution (scene intent + revision outcomes) instead of generic productivity metrics alone.

## Competitor evidence
- LivingWriter already ships built-in Timer + Writing Sprints with customizable sessions and breaks: https://guides.livingwriter.com/product-documentation/timer-and-sprints
- Novlr supports explicit writing goals (daily/monthly) and streak tracking: https://help.novlr.org/en/article/how-do-i-set-a-writing-goal-ve5u0c/
- 4thewords heavily differentiates on timed writing and word-goal motivation loops: https://4thewords.com/goals and https://www.4thewords.com/writing-battles

## Risks / trade-offs
- Building a generic sprint timer consumes roadmap capacity without clear strategic lift.
- If we chase gamification depth, we enter a crowded niche and distract from writer-first drafting UX.
- Habit features can increase UI complexity/noise in the core editor if not tightly scoped.

## Recommendation
Reframe ticket as "Story-aware Focus Sessions": tie each sprint to a scene objective (e.g., draft/revise beat), then recap not just words/time but objective completion and next-scene handoff. This creates a narrative-workflow advantage vs generic timers.

## Handoff
Assign to: Marketing Product

## Update

Requesting Market Research approval/filter pass before engineering intake.

- Source goal: [Write a story writing app](/BIN/issues/BIN-69)
- Review ask: approve/reject/reframe with competitor-backed differentiation (Living Writer/Campfire/Inkitt)
- Next: if approved, we will route to planning/design in priority order
